UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the VoyageFlow application. Authentication details are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number, optional for communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

TravelService.title: TravelService
TravelService.description: Represents a searchable travel service available through VoyageFlow, such as flights, hotels, or activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TravelService entity.

name.description: The name of the travel service (e.g., 'Hilton Hotel NYC', 'Flight to Paris').

description.description: A detailed description of the travel service.

type.description: The category of the travel service (e.g., 'flight', 'hotel', 'activity').

locationName.description: The geographical location associated with the service (e.g., 'Paris', 'New York City').

basePrice.description: The starting or base price of the service.

currency.description: The currency of the base price (e.g., 'USD', 'NGN').

provider.description: The company or entity providing the service (e.g., 'AirFrance', 'Booking.com').

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the travel service.

rating.description: An optional rating for the service, typically on a scale (e.g., 1-5).

BookingBasket.title: BookingBasket
BookingBasket.description: Represents a temporary collection of selected travel services by a user before proceeding to checkout.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingBasket entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this basket. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N BookingBasket)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the booking basket was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking basket was last updated.

BasketItem.title: BasketItem
BasketItem.description: Represents a single travel service added to a user's booking basket.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BasketItem entity.

basketId.description: Reference to the BookingBasket this item belongs to. (Relationship: BookingBasket 1:N BasketItem)

travelServiceId.description: Reference to the TravelService being added. (Relationship: TravelService 1:N BasketItem)

quantity.description: The number of units for the service (e.g., number of rooms, tickets).

priceAtTimeOfAddition.description: The price of the service per unit at the time it was added to the basket.

startDate.description: The start date and time for the service (e.g., flight departure, hotel check-in).

endDate.description: The end date and time for the service (e.g., flight arrival, hotel check-out).

paxAdults.description: The number of adult passengers or guests for this service.

paxChildren.description: The number of child passengers or guests for this service.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a confirmed travel booking made by a user through VoyageFlow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the booking. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Booking)

bookingReference.description: A unique, human-readable reference code for the booking.

totalAmount.description: The total amount paid for this booking across all its items.

currency.description: The currency of the total amount.

status.description: The current status of the booking (e.g., 'pending', 'confirmed', 'cancelled', 'completed').

bookingDate.description: Timestamp when the booking was confirmed.

paymentId.description: Reference to the Payment associated with this booking. (Relationship: Booking 1:1 Payment)

BookingItem.title: BookingItem
BookingItem.description: Represents a single travel service included within a confirmed booking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingItem entity.

bookingId.description: Reference to the Booking this item belongs to. (Relationship: Booking 1:N BookingItem)

travelServiceId.description: Reference to the TravelService that was booked. (Relationship: TravelService 1:N BookingItem)

quantity.description: The number of units for the service as part of the booking.

priceAtTimeOfBooking.description: The price of the service per unit at the time the booking was confirmed.

startDate.description: The confirmed start date and time for the service.

endDate.description: The confirmed end date and time for the service.

paxAdults.description: The number of adult passengers or guests for this booked service.

paxChildren.description: The number of child passengers or guests for this booked service.

itemStatus.description: The status of this specific item within the booking (e.g., 'confirmed', 'awaiting_confirmation', 'cancelled').

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records details of a payment transaction for a booking within VoyageFlow, integrated with Paystack.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

bookingId.description: Reference to the Booking associated with this payment. (Relationship: Booking 1:1 Payment)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the payment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Payment)

transactionId.description: The unique transaction identifier provided by the payment gateway (e.g., Paystack).

amount.description: The amount of money paid in this transaction.

currency.description: The currency of the payment amount.

paymentGateway.description: The name of the payment gateway used (e.g., 'Paystack').

paymentDate.description: Timestamp when the payment transaction occurred.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. Access is strictly limited to the user whose ID matches the document ID. This path ensures path-based ownership and simplifies rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Contains publicly searchable travel service listings (flights, hotels, activities). All authenticated users can read this collection, while write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: travelServiceId
params.description: The unique ID of the travel service.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to grant administrator privileges. The existence of a document for a specific userId signifies administrative rights for that user. This is an implicit entity, typically containing minimal data like a timestamp.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who possesses administrator privileges.

definition.description: Represents a user's temporary collection of selected travel services. Owned by the user specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this booking basket.

params.name: basketId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking basket.

definition.description: Represents a single travel service added to a user's booking basket. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent user's path for authorization independence, ensuring atomic security rule evaluations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent booking basket.

params.name: basketId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent booking basket.

params.name: basketItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the basket item.

definition.description: Represents a confirmed travel booking made by a user. Owned by the user specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who made this booking.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking.

definition.description: Represents a single travel service included within a confirmed booking. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent booking's path for authorization independence, ensuring atomic security rule evaluations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent booking.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent booking.

params.name: bookingItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking item.

definition.description: Records details of a payment transaction associated with a user and a booking. Owned by the user specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who initiated this payment.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique ID of the payment transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for VoyageFlow prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding user ownership directly into the document path for all user-specific data and denormalizing the `userId` into subcollection documents where the security rules would otherwise require a parent lookup. For example, `BasketItem` and `BookingItem` documents, residing in subcollections, will explicitly include a `userId` field that matches the `userId` in their ancestral path. This ensures that a document's authorization context is self-contained, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent ownership. This design makes read/write operations atomic from a rules perspective and greatly simplifies debugging.

**Structural Segregation and QAPs (Queryable Authorization Postures):**
1.  **User-Owned Private Data:** All data directly owned by a user (`UserProfile`, `BookingBasket`, `BasketItem`, `Booking`, `BookingItem`, `Payment`) is organized under `/users/{userId}/...`. This path-based ownership intrinsically scopes `list` operations (QAPs) such that a user can only query their own data. For instance, a query to `/users/{request.auth.uid}/bookings` will only return bookings belonging to the authenticated user, satisfying the QAP principle by not relying on rules to filter results after retrieval.
2.  **Public/Global Data:** `TravelService` data, which needs to be accessible for searching by all users, is placed in a top-level collection `/travel_services`. This allows any authenticated user to `list` and read available services globally, adhering to QAP by providing a collection with a homogeneous security posture (read-all authenticated, write-admin). This avoids mixing public and private data, simplifying rules significantly.
3.  **DBAC (Database Access Control):** Global roles, specifically 'admin' for managing `TravelService` data, are implemented using 'existence over content' in a dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{userId}`. The presence of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants admin privileges, enabling simple rule checks without custom claims.

**Access Modeling:** The design consistently uses hierarchical paths for user-owned data (`/users/{userId}/threads/{threadId}`) and continues this hierarchy for nested data (`/users/{userId}/threads/{threadId}/messages/{messageId}`). This pattern makes ownership explicit and security rules straightforward, focusing on `request.auth.uid` matching the path wildcard `userId` or the denormalized `userId` field.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:** Semantic wildcards (`{userId}`, `{basketId}`, `{bookingId}`) are used consistently. The `status` field in entities like `Booking` and `BookingItem` explicitly models their state, reducing ambiguity. Denormalized fields are explicitly mentioned in path descriptions to ensure developers are aware of their purpose and maintenance requirements.

Overall, this structure provides a robust foundation for VoyageFlow, ensuring security and maintainability of its Firestore data.
